Coronary Artery Disease.primary_symptoms: Chest pain or discomfort (Angina), Pain radiating to left arm/jaw/back, Shortness of breath on exertion
Coronary Artery Disease.red_flags: Crushing chest pain lasting >15 mins, Unconsciousness
Coronary Artery Disease.first_aid: Call Emergency (999), chew an Aspirin (if no allergy), sit down and stay calm.

Heart Failure.primary_symptoms: Severe shortness of breath when lying flat (Orthopnea), Swelling in legs/ankles/feet (Edema), Rapid or irregular heartbeat
Heart Failure.secondary_symptoms: Persistent cough with white/pink phlegm, Swelling of the abdomen (Ascites), Sudden weight gain
Heart Failure.red_flags: Foamy pink mucus, Severe inability to breathe
Heart Failure.first_aid: Sit upright to ease breathing, avoid physical exertion, seek immediate hospital care.

Arrhythmia.primary_symptoms: Fluttering in chest, Racing heartbeat (Tachycardia), Slow heartbeat (Bradycardia)
Arrhythmia.first_aid: Sit or lie down, try to relax, practice deep breathing. Call help if fainting occurs.

Stroke.primary_symptoms: Sudden numbness/weakness of face/arm/leg, Sudden confusion or trouble speaking, Sudden trouble seeing
Stroke.secondary_symptoms: Sudden severe headache, Loss of balance or coordination, Dizziness
Stroke.red_flags: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty (FAST)
Stroke.first_aid: FAST: Face, Arms, Speech, Time. Call Emergency immediately. Do NOT give food/water.

Migraine.primary_symptoms: Severe throbbing pain (one side), Sensitivity to light/sound, Nausea and vomiting
Migraine.secondary_symptoms: Visual aura (flashes of light), Tingling in face/hands, Lightheadedness
Migraine.red_flags: Worst headache of life (Thunderclap), Stiff neck with fever
Migraine.first_aid: Rest in a dark, quiet room. Apply cold compress to forehead. Hydrate.

Epilepsy.primary_symptoms: Uncontrollable jerking movements (Seizures), Loss of consciousness, Temporary confusion
Epilepsy.secondary_symptoms: Staring spells, Fear or anxiety (Aura), Psychic symptoms (Déjà vu)
Epilepsy.red_flags: Seizure lasting >5 mins, Second seizure immediately after first
Epilepsy.first_aid: Recovery Position: Turn person to side. Cushion head. Do NOT put anything in mouth. Time the seizure.

GERD (Acid Reflux).primary_symptoms: Burning sensation in chest (Heartburn), Regurgitation of food/sour liquid, Difficulty swallowing
GERD (Acid Reflux).secondary_symptoms: Chronic dry cough, Sore throat, Sensation of a lump in the throat
GERD (Acid Reflux).first_aid: Sit upright (do not lie down). Drink water or take an antacid. Avoid trigger foods.

Peptic Ulcer.primary_symptoms: Burning stomach pain, Pain worsens when stomach is empty, Feeling of fullness/bloating
Peptic Ulcer.secondary_symptoms: Intolerance to fatty foods, Nausea, Belching
Peptic Ulcer.first_aid: Avoid spicy/acidic foods and NSAID pain killers. Drink cool milk or water. See a doctor.

IBS.primary_symptoms: Abdominal pain or cramping, Bloating and gas, Diarrhea or Constipation (alternating)
IBS.secondary_symptoms: Mucus in stool, Urgency to have a bowel movement, Feeling of incomplete evacuation
IBS.red_flags: Rectal bleeding, Weight loss, Pain at night
IBS.first_aid: Hydrate appropriately. Eat small, bland meals. Avoid caffeine and stress.

Diabetes Mellitus (Type 2).primary_symptoms: Increased thirst (Polydipsia), Frequent urination (Polyuria), Unexplained weight loss
Diabetes Mellitus (Type 2).secondary_symptoms: Extreme hunger (Polyphagia), Blurred vision, Slow-healing sores, Fatigue
Diabetes Mellitus (Type 2).red_flags: Fruity breath odor, Confusion, Deep rapid breathing
Diabetes Mellitus (Type 2).first_aid: Drink water (sugar-free). If feeling faint (hypoglycemia), eat a sugary snack. Monitor glucose.

Hyperthyroidism.primary_symptoms: Unintentional weight loss, Rapid heartbeat (Tachycardia), Tremors in hands
Hyperthyroidism.secondary_symptoms: Sweating, Anxiety/Irritability, Heat sensitivity, Enlarged thyroid gland (Goiter)
Hyperthyroidism.red_flags: Fever with rapid pulse, Delirium (Thyroid Storm)
Hyperthyroidism.first_aid: Rest in a cool environment. Avoid caffeine. Seek medical advice for medication.

Hypothyroidism.primary_symptoms: Fatigue, Increased sensitivity to cold, Weight gain
Hypothyroidism.secondary_symptoms: Constipation, Dry skin, Puffy face, Hoarseness
Hypothyroidism.first_aid: Keep warm. Avoid heavy meals. Consult doctor for thyroid hormone replacement.

Eczema.primary_symptoms: Dry cracked skin, Itching (especially at night), Red to brownish-gray patches
Eczema.secondary_symptoms: Small raised bumps, Thickened scaly skin, Raw sensitive skin
Eczema.red_flags: Signs of infection (pus, yellow crusts, fever)
Eczema.first_aid: Apply moisturizer immediately. Avoid scratching. Use a cool, wet compress.

Psoriasis.primary_symptoms: Red patches of skin with silvery scales, Dry cracked skin that bleeds, Itching/burning
Psoriasis.secondary_symptoms: Thickened/pitted nails, Swollen and stiff joints
Psoriasis.first_aid: Moisturize heavily. Avoid harsh soaps. Expose affected skin to small amounts of sunlight.

Fungal Infection.primary_symptoms: Ring-shaped rash, Red scaly cracked skin, Hair loss in affected area
Fungal Infection.first_aid: Keep area dry and clean. Do not share towels. Apply over-the-counter antifungal cream.

Kidney Stones.primary_symptoms: Severe sharp pain in side/back, Pain radiating to lower abdomen/groin, Fluctuating pain intensity
Kidney Stones.secondary_symptoms: Pink/red/brown urine, Nausea and vomiting, Persistent need to urinate
Kidney Stones.red_flags: Fever and chills, Inability to pass urine
Kidney Stones.first_aid: Drink plenty of water to flush system. Take OTC pain relief. Seek help if pain is unbearable.

UTI.primary_symptoms: Strong persistent urge to urinate, Burning sensation when urinating, Cloudy urine
UTI.secondary_symptoms: Red or bright pink urine, Strong-smelling urine, Pelvic pain (women)
UTI.red_flags: Back pain with high fever (Kidney infection)
UTI.first_aid: Drink lots of water. Avoid caffeine/alcohol. Apply heat pad to abdomen for pain.

Rheumatoid Arthritis.primary_symptoms: Tender warm swollen joints, Joint stiffness (worse in mornings), Fatigue/fever
Rheumatoid Arthritis.secondary_symptoms: Symmetrical joint pain, Firm bumps under skin (Rheumatoid nodules)
Rheumatoid Arthritis.first_aid: Rest the joint. Apply warm or cold compress. Take anti-inflammatory medication.

Gout.primary_symptoms: Intense joint pain (often big toe), Lingering discomfort, Inflammation and redness
Gout.secondary_symptoms: Limited range of motion, Warmth over the joint
Gout.first_aid: Elevate the joint. Keep weight off it. Apply ice packs. Drink water. Avoid alcohol/red meat.

Asthma.primary_symptoms: Shortness of breath, Chest tightness or pain, Wheezing when exhaling
Asthma.secondary_symptoms: Trouble sleeping due to breathlessness, Coughing/wheezing attacks
Asthma.red_flags: Rapid worsening of breath, No improvement with inhaler
Asthma.first_aid: Sit upright. Use rescue inhaler (usually blue) immediately. Loosen tight clothing.

COPD.primary_symptoms: Chronic cough, Shortness of breath during activity, Frequent respiratory infections
COPD.secondary_symptoms: Blueness of lips/fingernails, Fatigue, High mucus production
COPD.first_aid: Stop smoking/irritants immediately. Use prescribed inhaler. Pursed-lip breathing technique.

Dengue Fever.primary_symptoms: High fever (40°C/104°F), Severe headache, Pain behind the eyes
Dengue Fever.secondary_symptoms: Joint and muscle pain, Rash, Mild bleeding (nose/gum)
Dengue Fever.red_flags: Severe abdominal pain, Persistent vomiting, Bleeding gums
Dengue Fever.first_aid: Do NOT take Ibuprofen/Aspirin. Take Paracetamol. Drink plenty of fluids (ORS/Water). Rest.

Tuberculosis (TB).primary_symptoms: Coughing for 3+ weeks, Coughing up blood/mucus, Chest pain
Tuberculosis (TB).secondary_symptoms: Unintentional weight loss, Fatigue, Fever, Night sweats
Tuberculosis (TB).red_flags: Massive coughing of blood, Difficulty breathing
Tuberculosis (TB).first_aid: Wear a mask to protect others. Isolate from family. Seek immediate medical testing.
